name: cal-com-share-meeting-links
title: Cal.com
description: Manage your Cal.com schedules, bookings, out-of-office, and meeting links from Raycast
author: eluce2

commands.name: view-availability
commands.title: View Availability
commands.subtitle: Cal.com
commands.description: View and manage your Cal.com availability schedules

commands.name: out-of-office
commands.title: Out of Office
commands.subtitle: Cal.com
commands.description: View and manage your Cal.com out-of-office entries

commands.name: create-out-of-office
commands.title: Create Out of Office
commands.subtitle: Cal.com
commands.description: Schedule a new out-of-office entry (with draft support)

tools.name: get-me
tools.title: Get My Profile
tools.description: Get the authenticated user's Cal.com profile (id, username, name, email, timezone, default schedule id, organization).

tools.name: update-me
tools.title: Update My Profile
tools.description: Update the authenticated user's profile (name, username, bio, timezone, week start, time format, locale). Shows a confirmation before applying.

tools.name: get-event-types
tools.title: List Event Types
tools.description: List event types (booking links) for a user. Each event type has a numeric id, slug, title, lengthInMinutes, locations, and bookingUrl.

tools.name: get-event-type
tools.title: Get Event Type
tools.description: Get a single event type by numeric id.

tools.name: create-event-type
tools.title: Create Event Type
tools.description: Create a new event type (booking link). Requires title and lengthInMinutes. Shows a confirmation.

tools.name: update-event-type
tools.title: Update Event Type
tools.description: Update fields on an event type by id. Shows a confirmation listing the changes.

tools.name: delete-event-type
tools.title: Delete Event Type
tools.description: Permanently delete an event type. Shows a confirmation.

tools.name: get-bookings
tools.title: List Bookings
tools.description: List bookings with filters (status, date range, attendee, event type). Returns slim records (uid, title, start, end, hosts, attendees, status, meetingUrl). Use this before any per-booking action to look up the uid.

tools.name: get-booking
tools.title: Get Booking
tools.description: Get a single booking by uid (look up via get-bookings if unknown).

tools.name: create-booking
tools.title: Create Booking
tools.description: Create a booking on an event type. Requires eventTypeId, start (ISO 8601), and the attendee's name, email, and IANA timezone. Shows a confirmation.

tools.name: reschedule-booking
tools.title: Reschedule Booking
tools.description: Reschedule a booking to a new ISO 8601 start time. Shows a confirmation. Look up the bookingUid via get-bookings first.

tools.name: cancel-booking
tools.title: Cancel Booking
tools.description: Cancel a booking by uid with a reason shared with attendees. Shows a confirmation.

tools.name: confirm-booking
tools.title: Confirm Booking
tools.description: Confirm a pending booking that requires manual confirmation. Shows a confirmation.

tools.name: mark-booking-absent
tools.title: Mark Booking No-Show
tools.description: Mark a booking (or specific attendees/host) as no-show. Shows a confirmation.

tools.name: get-booking-attendees
tools.title: List Booking Attendees
tools.description: Get all attendees for a booking by uid.

tools.name: add-booking-attendee
tools.title: Add Booking Attendee
tools.description: Add an attendee (name, email, IANA timezone) to an existing booking. Shows a confirmation.

tools.name: get-booking-attendee
tools.title: Get Booking Attendee
tools.description: Get a specific attendee by id within a booking.

tools.name: get-schedules
tools.title: List Schedules
tools.description: List all availability schedules. Each schedule has a name, timezone, isDefault flag, weekly availability rules, and date overrides.

tools.name: get-schedule
tools.title: Get Schedule
tools.description: Get a single availability schedule by id.

tools.name: get-default-schedule
tools.title: Get Default Schedule
tools.description: Get the user's default availability schedule.

tools.name: create-schedule
tools.title: Create Schedule
tools.description: Create a new availability schedule (name, IANA timezone, optional weekly availability + overrides). Shows a confirmation.

tools.name: update-schedule
tools.title: Update Schedule
tools.description: Update fields on a schedule (name, timeZone, isDefault, availability, overrides). Shows a confirmation listing changes.

tools.name: delete-schedule
tools.title: Delete Schedule
tools.description: Permanently delete a schedule. Shows a confirmation.

tools.name: get-availability
tools.title: Get Available Slots
tools.description: Get available time slots for an event type within a window. Requires eventTypeId, start, end (ISO 8601). Optional duration, timeZone (IANA), username.

tools.name: get-busy-times
tools.title: Get Busy Times
tools.description: Get busy intervals across the user's connected calendars within an ISO 8601 window.

tools.name: get-conferencing-apps
tools.title: List Conferencing Apps
tools.description: List connected conferencing apps (Zoom, Google Meet, etc.).

tools.name: calculate-routing-form-slots
tools.title: Calculate Routing Form Slots
tools.description: Calculate available slots based on a routing form response. Requires routingFormId, response (field-id → value map), start, end.

tools.name: get-org-memberships
tools.title: List Organization Memberships
tools.description: List memberships for an organization (requires admin/owner role).

tools.name: create-org-membership
tools.title: Create Organization Membership
tools.description: Add a user to an organization with a role (OWNER/ADMIN/MEMBER). Shows a confirmation.

tools.name: get-org-membership
tools.title: Get Organization Membership
tools.description: Get a single organization membership by id.

tools.name: delete-org-membership
tools.title: Remove Organization Membership
tools.description: Remove a user from an organization. Shows a confirmation.

tools.name: get-org-routing-forms
tools.title: List Organization Routing Forms
tools.description: List routing forms for an organization.

tools.name: get-org-routing-form-responses
tools.title: Get Routing Form Responses
tools.description: Get responses submitted to an organization's routing form.

ai.instructions:
This extension exposes Cal.com's v2 API surface — the same surface as Cal.com's official MCP server (mcp.cal.com), but via the user's existing API key so no OAuth handshake is needed.

General rules:
- ALWAYS look up booking UIDs via get-bookings before calling cancel-booking, reschedule-booking, confirm-booking, mark-booking-absent, get-booking-attendees, add-booking-attendee, or get-booking. Never invent or guess a uid. If the user references a meeting by description (e.g. "my 2pm tomorrow", "meeting with John"), call get-bookings first with appropriate filters and disambiguate from the results.
- ALWAYS use the booking `uid` (a string like "abc123xyz") for per-booking actions — NOT the numeric `id`. The two are different.
- ALWAYS pass timezones in IANA format (e.g. "America/Los_Angeles", "Europe/Berlin"). Never abbreviations like "PST".
- ALWAYS pass datetimes as ISO 8601 with explicit offset or `Z` (e.g. "2026-06-01T14:00:00-07:00" or "2026-06-01T21:00:00Z"). Never naive local strings.

Booking lookup tips:
- For "this week" / "next week" queries, use get-bookings with `status: "upcoming"` and filter the results client-side by start range — the API does not need explicit date params for short windows.
- For "past meetings with X" queries, use get-bookings with `status: "past"` and `attendeeName` or `attendeeEmail`.
- For pending approvals, use `status: "unconfirmed"`.

Availability / scheduling:
- get-availability needs an eventTypeId. Call get-event-types first if the user hasn't named a specific type — pick the best match by length ("30 min chat" → 30-min event type).
- For "when am I free Monday", use get-availability with start=Monday 00:00 in the user's timezone and end=Monday 23:59. Default the user's timezone via get-me if not specified.

Write-tool confirmations:
- All create/update/delete/cancel/reschedule/mark/add tools show a confirmation dialog before executing. Present the user with the full action context so they can verify (e.g. "Cancel your 2pm meeting with john@example.com on June 5?") — do not call the tool blindly.

Organization tools:
- Only call org-* tools if the user is in an organization (get-me returns organization.id when applicable). If they aren't, surface a clear message instead of guessing an organizationId.

OOO note:
- The extension's Raycast commands (Out of Office, Create Out of Office) handle OOO. Out-of-office is not part of Cal.com's MCP server tool surface, so it is not exposed as an AI tool here — direct the user to the OOO commands for those workflows.

evals.input: @cal-com-share-meeting-links what bookings do I have this week?
@cal-com-share-meeting-links what event types do I have?
@cal-com-share-meeting-links show me available slots for my 30 minute event type next Monday from 9am to 5pm Pacific time

preferences.name: region
preferences.title: Server Region
preferences.description: Choose your Cal.com server region. Use Europe if your account is on cal.eu.
